## Summary

Syncs content from the private repo to public. Two commits.

### Commit 1: SRE README + minor fixes

**`claude_agent_sdk/README.md`**
- Add Notebook 03 (Site Reliability Agent) section — the cookbook itself was published in #391 but the README was never updated to mention it
- Add `site_reliability_agent/` to the Complete Agent Implementations list

**`CLAUDE.md`**

### Commit 2: Notebook 04 — Migrating from the OpenAI Agents SDK

New cookbook that walks through porting an OpenAI Agents SDK app to the Claude Agent SDK, mapping each primitive (tools, guardrails, sessions, handoffs) through a single expense-approval agent example.

Sourced from the approved private-repo PR (`preston/migration`, 7 commits of review iteration).

Companion public→private sync: anthropics/claude-cookbooks-private#38

title: Fixed(contextual-embeddings): prepend context to chunk instead of appending
body:
Fixes #95 
cc @PedramNavid @FlorianPydde

## Root Cause
Corrects the order of concatenation in `text_to_embed` inside 
`capabilities/contextual-embeddings/guide.ipynb`.

## fixes
The Anthropic blog post on Contextual Embeddings describes the technique 
as **prepending** context to each chunk. The notebook had this reversed — 
the chunk appeared first, context second. This inconsistency can mislead 
readers learning the pattern.

## Change
**Before:**
'text_to_embed': f"{chunk['content']}\n\n{contextualized_text}"

**After:**
'text_to_embed': f"{contextualized_text}\n\n{chunk['content']}"
author: DhruvTilva

reviews.body: LGTM.

body:
## Summary
Updates relative paths in the SRE Incident Response Agent notebook to absolute GitHub URLs so they work correctly when rendered on platform.claude.com.

## Changes
- Updated `infra_setup.py` and `sre_mcp_server.py` links to point to the GitHub repository
- Fixed architecture diagram image path to use `raw.githubusercontent.com` URL

## Why
The cookbook is live at https://platform.claude.com/cookbook/claude-agent-sdk-03-the-site-reliability-agent but the relative links were broken because the platform cannot resolve paths against the local filesystem.
author: benlehrburger-ant

reviews.body:
## PR Review

**Recommendation**: COMMENT

### Summary
This PR correctly fixes broken links in the SRE cookbook by replacing relative file paths and `attachment:` image references with absolute GitHub URLs so they render properly on platform.claude.com. The fix is well-scoped and accurate.

<details>
<summary>Actionable Feedback (2 items)</summary>

- [ ] `03_The_site_reliability_agent.ipynb` (in the cell with `![SRE Agent Architecture](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/...)`) — The `attachments` key with the base64-encoded PNG blob is still present in this cell JSON metadata even though the source now uses a remote URL. The embedded attachment is now dead weight (not referenced by the `source` field), adding unnecessary file size to the notebook. Consider removing the `attachments` dict from that cell's metadata to keep the notebook lean.

- [ ] General: The file links use `/tree/` in the GitHub URL. For single files, GitHub redirects this to `/blob/`, so it works — but `/blob/` is the conventional URL for viewing a specific file. Very minor style point, not a blocker.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Detailed Review</summary>

### Correctness
All three sets of URLs are correct and point to existing files. Using `raw.githubusercontent.com` for the image is the right call — `github.com/tree/` serves an HTML page, not raw bytes, so it would break the inline markdown image render.

### Consistency
All markdown link occurrences of both files were updated across both array-format and single-string `source` cells. Runtime code cell references (bare filenames used to locate files at execution time) are correctly left unchanged.

### Security
No concerns. All URLs point to the expected `anthropics` organization.

### Positive Notes
- Tightly scoped fix with no unrelated modifications
- All occurrences caught — both array-format and single-string source cells updated correctly
- Correct use of `raw.githubusercontent.com` for image vs `github.com/tree/` for file links shows g

reviews.body:
## PR Review

**Recommendation**: COMMENT

### Summary
This PR adds a well-crafted Site Reliability Agent cookbook demonstrating a multi-tool Claude agent for incident investigation and remediation. The overall code quality, security model, and pedagogical structure are strong. Several items need attention before merging, primarily around documentation clarity and hardening of the example code.

<details>
<summary>Actionable Feedback (9 items)</summary>

- [ ] `claude_agent_sdk/03_The_site_reliability_agent.ipynb` (cell with `run_shell_command`) — The inline `run_shell_command` only checks `args[0] in ("docker-compose", "docker")` but does not validate subcommands. The MCP server (`sre_mcp_server.py`) correctly adds a subcommand allowlist. Since the notebook is where readers learn the pattern, add the same allowlist here or add a prose note clarifying this is a simplified version and that the production implementation is in `sre_mcp_server.py`.

- [ ] `claude_agent_sdk/site_reliability_agent/examples/sre_bot_slack.py` (~line 114) — PagerDuty webhook authentication via `X-PagerDuty-Signature` is not implemented. The docstring mentions this, but since the cookbook may be deployed directly, add a commented-out skeleton or a `raise NotImplementedError` guard at the exact point where the HMAC check should go, so readers cannot miss it.

- [ ] `claude_agent_sdk/03_The_site_reliability_agent.ipynb` (cell with `run_shell_command`) — `subprocess.run` is used synchronously inside `async def` functions. This blocks the event loop during the 60-second timeout. Either use `asyncio.create_subprocess_exec` (as done correctly in `sre_mcp_server.py`), or add a prose note that this is a simplified synchronous version.

- [ ] `claude_agent_sdk/site_reliability_agent/sre_mcp_server.py` (~lines 2161–2165) — `search_term` is interpolated into a Confluence CQL query with only quote escaping. CQL has additional injection vectors (e.g., unbalanced parentheses). Apply a strict character allo

title: docs(misc): update prompt caching cookbook with automatic caching
body:
# Pull Request

Update for automatic prompt caching
author: PedramNavid

reviews.body:
## PR Review

**Recommendation**: COMMENT

### Summary
This PR updates the prompt caching cookbook to document the new automatic caching feature alongside existing explicit breakpoints. The restructuring into three examples (automatic single-turn, automatic multi-turn, explicit breakpoints) is pedagogically sound, and the implemented code is correct and idiomatic.

<details>
<summary>Actionable Feedback (4 items)</summary>

- [ ] `misc/prompt_caching.ipynb` (install cell with `%pip install --upgrade 'anthropic>=0.83.0'`) — The notebook pins `anthropic>=0.83.0`, but `pyproject.toml` still specifies `anthropic>=0.71.0`. A CI environment that resolves deps from `pyproject.toml` alone could install a version that lacks the top-level `cache_control` parameter. Run `uv add 'anthropic>=0.83.0'` to update the project baseline so the lockfile and `pyproject.toml` stay in sync.

- [ ] `misc/prompt_caching.ipynb` (opening markdown cell) — Introduction jumps straight to the feature overview without first hooking on the problem or listing what the reader will accomplish. Consider adding 2–4 outcome-focused bullet points, e.g. "By the end of this cookbook, you'll be able to: enable automatic caching with a one-line change; use caching in multi-turn conversations without managing markers; choose between automatic and explicit breakpoints."

- [ ] `misc/prompt_caching.ipynb` (last cell — "Choosing an approach" table) — The notebook ends without a conclusion cell. Add a short closing markdown cell summarising the three examples and pointing readers to next steps (e.g. applying the `TIMESTAMP` cache-buster pattern in their own projects).

- [ ] `misc/prompt_caching.ipynb` (install cell with `%pip install --upgrade ...`) — Using `--quiet` still emits the "Note: you may need to restart the kernel" notice. The project convention is `%%capture` at the top of the cell to suppress all pip output in stored notebook outputs.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Detailed Review</summary>

### Code

- Choose appropriate compaction strategies (traditional vs. instant) based on your use case

title: feat: add notebook testing scaffold with pytest and tox
body: ## Human Written Summary I noticed some issues with notebooks including some that lack an output for a code cell, or cells that were run in the wrong order. This can cause [subtle](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7jiPeIFXb6U) issues. I created a test suite that validates all cells have been executed, and in order, and cells start from 1. It ensures no errors, no hardcoded API keys, and is valid JSON. This is integrated with both pytest and tox allowing for easy invocation and isolation (important to ensure that dependencies are correctly identified in the notebook) as well for parallel testing. A Github action runs on all new and changed notebooks. There are two types of tests: * fast - requires no execution and is static analysis. Runs for everyone on GHA * slow - requires code execution, ensuring that the notebook itself completes successfully. Only runs for people authorized (MEMBER/OWNER) to write in this repo. Note slow is *SLOW* as some notebooks can take 10-15 minutes to complete each. I wouldn't recommend running it locally. Also note: many notebooks are failing these tests. That's ok! I will fix them over time, but the key is to ensure new/changed notebooks no longer fail these tests. ## Computer Written Summary ## Usage ```bash # Run all structural tests (fast, no API calls) make test-notebooks # Test a specific notebook make test-notebooks NOTEBOOK=tool_use/calculator_tool.ipynb # Test notebooks in a directory make test-notebooks NOTEBOOK_DIR=capabilities # Run in isolated tox environment make test-notebooks-tox # Actually execute notebooks (requires API key) make test-notebooks-exec ``` ## Test plan - [x] Tests run successfully on individual notebooks - [x] CLI runner works with `--quick`, `--list`, `--notebook` options - [x] Tox environments created successfully - [x] Linting passes on all new code - [ ] GitHub Actions workflow runs on PR (will verify after opening) 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: PedramNavid

title: Upgrade GitHub Actions for Node 24 compatibility
body:
## Summary

Upgrade GitHub Actions to their latest versions to ensure compatibility with Node 24, as Node 20 will reach end-of-life in April 2026.

## Changes

| Action | Old Version(s) | New Version | Release | Files |
|--------|---------------|-------------|---------|-------|
| `actions/checkout` | [`v4`](https://github.com/actions/checkout/releases/tag/v4) | [`v6`](https://github.com/actions/checkout/releases/tag/v6) | [Release](https://github.com/actions/checkout/releases/tag/v6) | claude-link-review.yml, claude-model-check.yml, claude-pr-review.yml, links.yml, lint-format.yml, notebook-diff-comment.yml, notebook-quality.yml, verify-authors.yml |
| `actions/setup-python` | [`v5`](https://github.com/actions/setup-python/releases/tag/v5) | [`v6`](https://github.com/actions/setup-python/releases/tag/v6) | [Release](https://github.com/actions/setup-python/releases/tag/v6) | links.yml, verify-authors.yml |
| `actions/upload-artifact` | [`v4`](https://github.com/actions/upload-artifact/releases/tag/v4) | [`v6`](https://github.com/actions/upload-artifact/releases/tag/v6) | [Release](https://github.com/actions/upload-artifact/releases/tag/v6) | links.yml, notebook-quality.yml |

## Context

Per [GitHub's announcement](https://github.blog/changelog/2025-09-19-deprecation-of-node-20-on-github-actions-runners/), Node 20 is being deprecated and runners will begin using Node 24 by default starting March 4th, 2026.

### Why this matters

- **Node 20 EOL**: April 2026
- **Node 24 default**: March 4th, 2026
- **Action**: Update to latest action versions that support Node 24

### Security Note

Actions that were previously pinned to commit SHAs remain pinned to SHAs (updated to the latest release SHA) to maintain the security benefits of immutable references.

### Testing

These changes only affect CI/CD workflow configurations and should not impact application functionality. The workflows should be tested by running them on a branch before merging.
author: salmanmkc

refactor(ci): use --no-commit and git restore for cleaner workflow exclusion

Replace the checkout + amend approach with a cleaner solution:

**Old approach (from PR #15):**
```bash
git merge public/main --no-edit
git checkout HEAD^1 -- .github/workflows/
git add .github/workflows/
git commit --amend --no-edit
```

**New approach:**
```bash
git merge public/main --no-commit --no-ff
git restore --source=HEAD --staged --worktree .github/workflows/
git commit --no-edit -m "Merge changes from public repository"
```

**Why this is better:**
- No commit amending needed (we fix before committing)
- No HEAD^1 reference (clearer to use HEAD for pre-merge state)
- Single atomic restore command (handles staging + worktree)
- More explicit intent (--no-commit says "let me fix first")
- Handles both modified AND new workflow files

The git restore command with --staged --worktree ensures both the
staging area and working tree are reset to their pre-merge state,
removing all workflow changes (including new files like lint-format.yml).

title: fix(ci): include all markdown files in lychee link check
body:
# Pull Request

## Description

This PR fixes the lychee link checker CI workflow to check **all changed markdown files** regardless of their directory location.

**Problem:** The previous implementation only checked markdown files in the `skills/` directory and the root `README.md`. This caused CI failures when PRs modified markdown files in other directories like `third_party/`, `multimodal/`, etc.

**Example failure:** PR #263 modified `third_party/ElevenLabs/README.md`, but the file wasn't included in the link check, resulting in an empty file list and a lychee failure.

**Solution:** Updated the file list preparation logic to include all changed markdown files using a simple `.md$` pattern instead of restrictive directory-specific patterns.

## Type of Change

- [ ] New cookbook
- [x] Bug fix (fixes an issue in existing cookbook)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Code quality improvement (refactoring, optimization)
- [ ] Dependency update
- [x] Other (please describe): CI configuration fix

## Cookbook Checklist (if applicable)

N/A - This is a CI configuration change, not a cookbook change.

## Testing

- [x] I have tested this cookbook/change locally
- [x] All cells execute without errors

**Testing notes:**
- The CI workflow will run on this PR and should successfully check the changed `.yml` file
- No Python/notebook linters will run since only a YAML file was modified
- The fix ensures markdown files in any directory are included in future link checks

## Additional Context

**Changes made:**
- Updated `.github/workflows/links.yml:82-86` to use `grep '\.md$'` instead of `grep '^skills/.*\.md$'`
- Removed redundant explicit `README.md` check (now covered by general pattern)

**Impact:**
- Fixes: CI failure in PR #263
- Prevents: Similar failures for markdown files in any directory
- Maintains: All existing functionality for `skills/` and root README files

title: Add ElevenLabs Low Latency Voice Assistant Integration
body:
# Pull Request

## Description

This PR adds a comprehensive cookbook demonstrating how to build a low-latency voice assistant by integrating ElevenLabs' speech processing capabilities with Claude's conversational AI. The cookbook progressively optimizes for real-time performance, teaching developers how to minimize latency through various streaming techniques.

**What this cookbook demonstrates:**
- Integration of ElevenLabs speech-to-text and text-to-speech APIs with Claude
- Step-by-step optimization techniques to reduce latency in voice applications
- Comparison of different streaming approaches (HTTP streaming vs WebSocket streaming)
- Production-ready implementation of a continuous conversational voice assistant

## Type of Change

- [x] New cookbook
- [ ] Bug fix (fixes an issue in existing cookbook)

- [x] Includes expected outputs or results

- [x] All cells execute without errors

## Additional Context

This cookbook includes two main components:

1. **Interactive Notebook** (`low_latency_stt_claude_tts.ipynb`) - A tutorial-style notebook that walks through building a voice assistant step-by-step, demonstrating various optimization techniques with performance metrics at each stage.

2. **Production WebSocket Script** (`stream_voice_assistant_websocket.py`) - A fully functional voice assistant using WebSocket streaming for minimal latency, featuring continuous microphone input and gapless audio playback.

The cookbook is particularly valuable for developers building real-time voice applications who need to understand the tradeoffs between different streaming approaches and how to optimize for latency.

**Key features:**
- Compatible with ElevenLabs free tier
- Includes comprehensive setup instructions and environment configuration
- Well-documented code with security best practices (environment variables, input validation)
- Performance comparisons between different implementation approaches

feat(security): add detect-secrets configuration and Anthropic credentials detector

Add baseline configuration for the detect-secrets library with a custom plugin
to detect Anthropic API keys and credentials in notebooks. Includes comprehensive
set of built-in detectors and heuristic filters to prevent secrets from being
committed to the repository.

feat(cookbook-audit): integrate detect-secrets for hardcoded credential detection

Enhanced the notebook validation to use detect-secrets for identifying
hardcoded API keys and credentials. The implementation:
- Runs detect-secrets-hook on notebooks with baseline configuration
- Automatically locates baseline at `scripts/detect-secrets/.secrets.baseline`
- Falls back to basic pattern matching if detect-secrets unavailable
- Provides detailed output for manual review of potential secrets

Updated documentation to reflect the automated secret scanning capability.

chore(workflows): remove unnecessary id-token permission

Remove id-token: write permission from Claude Code workflow files
as it is not needed for these operations. The workflows only require:
- contents: read (to checkout repository code)
- pull-requests: write (to comment on pull requests)

The id-token: write permission is used for OIDC authentication with
cloud providers (AWS, GCP, Azure) which these workflows do not use.

This follows the principle of least privilege and reduces the
security attack surface.

- claude-notebook-review.yml
- claude-link-review.yml

title: Update legacy models across non-legacy cookbooks
body:
# Update legacy models across non-legacy cookbooks

## Summary

This PR updates all non-legacy cookbooks to use the latest non-legacy Claude model names, replacing legacy versioned identifiers with the new naming convention. Additionally, updates all references from `claude-code-sdk` to `claude-agent-sdk` to reflect the official package rebranding, including renaming the entire directory from `claude_code_sdk/` to `claude_agent_sdk/`.

## Model Updates

### Haiku Model Migration
**Legacy:** `claude-3-haiku-20240307`
**Updated to:** `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`

**Files affected:**
- `capabilities/classification/evaluation/promptfooconfig.yaml` (5 instances)
- `capabilities/classification/guide.ipynb`
- `capabilities/contextual-embeddings/guide.ipynb`
- `capabilities/retrieval_augmented_generation/evaluation/promptfooconfig_end_to_end.yaml`
- `capabilities/retrieval_augmented_generation/guide.ipynb`
- `capabilities/summarization/evaluation/promptfooconfig.yaml`
- `capabilities/summarization/guide.ipynb`
- `capabilities/text_to_sql/evaluation/promptfooconfig.yaml`
- `capabilities/text_to_sql/guide.ipynb`
- All evaluation scripts in `capabilities/retrieval_augmented_generation/evaluation/` (eval_end_to_end.py, prompts.py, provider_retrieval.py)
- `capabilities/summarization/evaluation/custom_evals/llm_eval.py`

### Sonnet Model Migration
**Legacy:** `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022`
**Updated to:** `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`

**Files affected:**
- `capabilities/retrieval_augmented_generation/guide.ipynb`
- `capabilities/summarization/guide.ipynb`
- `capabilities/text_to_sql/guide.ipynb`
- `capabilities/summarization/evaluation/promptfooconfig.yaml`
- `capabilities/text_to_sql/evaluation/promptfooconfig.yaml`
- All evaluation scripts in respective capability folders

### Opus Model Migration
**Legacy:** `claude-3-opus-20240229`
**Updated to:** `claude-opus-4-1-20250805`

**Files affected:**
- `misc/batch_processing.ipynb`
- `misc/building_evals.ipynb`
- `misc/building_moderation_filter.ipynb`
- `misc/generate_test_cases.ipynb`
- `misc/how_to_enable_json_mode.ipynb`
- `misc/metaprompt.ipynb`
- `misc/using_citations.ipynb`
- `multimodal/best_practices_for_vision.ipynb`
- `multimodal/getting_started_with_vision.ipynb`
- `multimodal/how_to_transcribe_text.ipynb`
- `multimodal/reading_charts_graphs_powerpoints.ipynb`
- `multimodal/using_sub_agents.ipynb`
- `observability/usage_cost_api.ipynb`
- `tool_use/calculator_tool.ipynb`
- `tool_use/customer_service_agent.ipynb`
- `tool_use/extracting_structured_json.ipynb`
- `tool_use/tool_use_with_pydantic.ipynb`
- `tool_use/vision_with_tools.ipynb`
- `third_party/LlamaIndex/` notebooks (all 5 notebooks)
- `third_party/MongoDB/rag_using_mongodb.ipynb`

## SDK Renaming Updates

### Directory Rename
**Old:** `claude_code_sdk/`
**New:** `claude_agent_sdk/`

The entire directory has been renamed to reflect the official package rebrand from "Claude Code SDK" to "Claude Agent SDK" as confirmed by:
- Official GitHub repository: [`anthropics/claude-agent-sdk-python`](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-agent-sdk-python)
- Official PyPI package: [`claude-agent-sdk`](https://pypi.org/project/claude-agent-sdk/)
- Official documentation: [Claude Agent SDK docs](https://docs.claude.com/en/api/agent-sdk/overview)

### Code Changes
Updated all references from `claude-code-sdk` to `claude-agent-sdk`:

**Files affected:**
- `claude_agent_sdk/README.md` - Updated installation instructions, description, and directory path
- `claude_agent_sdk/pyproject.toml` - Updated package name reference
- `claude_agent_sdk/00_The_one_liner_research_agent.ipynb` - Updated import statements
- `claude_agent_sdk/01_The_chief_of_staff_agent.ipynb` - Updated import statements
- `claude_agent_sdk/02_The_observability_agent.ipynb` - Updated import statements
- `claude_agent_sdk/chief_of_staff_agent/agent.py` - Changed `ClaudeCodeOptions` to `ClaudeAgentOptions`
- `claude_agent_sdk/observability_agent/agent.py` - Changed `ClaudeCodeOptions` to `ClaudeAgentOptions`
- `claude_agent_sdk/research_agent/agent.py` - Updated import statements
- `claude_agent_sdk/observability_agent/docker/Dockerfile` - Updated package references

**Class/Function Renames:**
- `ClaudeCodeOptions` → `ClaudeAgentOptions` (throughout all agent files)
- Package import: `from claude_code_sdk` → `from claude_agent_sdk`

## Third-Party Integration Updates

### Pinecone
- **Deleted:** `third_party/Pinecone/claude_3_rag_agent.ipynb`
- **Created:** `third_party/Pinecone/claude_rag_agent.ipynb`
- Updated to use non-legacy model names throughout

### Other Third-Party Integrations
- `third_party/Deepgram/prerecorded_audio.ipynb` - Updated model references
- `third_party/LlamaIndex/` - All 5 notebooks updated (Basic_RAG, Multi_Document_Agents, ReAct_Agent, Router_Query_Engine, SubQuestion_Query_Engine)
- `third_party/MongoDB/rag_using_mongodb.ipynb` - Comprehensive model updates (614 lines changed)

## Code Quality Improvements

Throughout all modified files, the following improvements were made:
- Improved code formatting and consistency
- Better import organization (alphabetized, properly separated)
- Enhanced readability with consistent spacing and line breaks
- Updated string formatting for better consistency
- Fixed trailing whitespace issues

## Files by Category

**Claude Agent SDK (41 files renamed + 9 files modified):**
- Entire directory structure renamed from `claude_code_sdk/` to `claude_agent_sdk/`
- All agent tutorials and supporting files updated
- Complete rebranding of package references throughout

**Capabilities (13 files):**
- Classification, Contextual Embeddings, RAG, Summarization, Text-to-SQL guides and evaluations

**Misc (12 files):**
- Batch processing, building evals, moderation filters, JSON mode, SQL queries, metaprompt, PDF summarization, prompt caching (standard and speculative), citations, web page reading

**Multimodal (5 files):**
- Vision best practices, getting started with vision, text transcription, chart/graph/PowerPoint reading, sub-agents

**Tool Use (5 files):**
- Calculator, customer service agent, structured JSON extraction, Pydantic integration, vision with tools

**Observability (1 file):**
- Usage cost API (877 lines changed - major refactor)

**Patterns (1 file):**
- Agents utility functions

**Third-Party (9 files):**
- Deepgram, LlamaIndex (5 notebooks), MongoDB, Pinecone (renamed)

## Statistics

- **Total files changed:** 87 (includes 41 renamed files + 55 content updates, with overlap)
- **Total insertions:** 6,656 lines
- **Total deletions:** 5,978 lines
- **Net change:** +678 lines

## Breaking Changes

### Directory Structure
Users cloning or updating this repository will need to update their paths:
- Old path: `anthropic-cookbook/claude_code_sdk`
- New path: `anthropic-cookbook/claude_agent_sdk`

### Installation Instructions
Updated to reflect official package naming:
```bash
# Old (deprecated)
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code-sdk

# New (current)
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk
```

## Testing Notes

All model references have been systematically updated to use the latest non-legacy model identifiers. Code examples should continue to work as expected with the new model names, maintaining backward compatibility in terms of functionality while using the updated naming convention.

The directory rename aligns with Anthropic's official rebranding of the package from "Claude Code SDK" to "Claude Agent SDK" to better reflect its broader capabilities beyond coding tasks.

---

**Note:** The file `misc/illustrated_responses.ipynb` was excluded from this PR due to GitHub's 5MB file size limit (file is 7.8MB).

title: Fix context management for SDK 0.69.0 and improve documentation
body:
## Summary

Fixes the memory cookbook to work with the latest anthropic SDK (0.69.0) and improves documentation around context clearing behavior.

## Changes

### SDK 0.69.0 Compatibility
- **Pass `context_management` as direct parameter** instead of `extra_body`
  - SDK 0.69.0 now accepts `context_management` as a top-level parameter
  - Updated `demo_helpers.py` and `code_review_demo.py`
- **Update response handling for Pydantic objects**
  - Use `getattr()` instead of `.get()` for accessing response attributes
  - `context_management` response is now a Pydantic model, not a dict

### Context Clearing Configuration
- **Lower `clear_at_least` threshold from 3000 to 50 tokens**
  - Memory tool operations have small results (~50-150 tokens each)
  - Previous threshold was too high, preventing clearing from triggering
  - Add inline documentation explaining the low threshold
- **Add explanatory notes in notebook**
  - Explain why token savings are small in this demo
  - Provide guidance for production configurations
  - Document that larger tool results would save more tokens

## Testing

Tested with anthropic SDK 0.69.0. Context clearing now triggers correctly:
- Clears 2 tool uses saving ~66 tokens per clearing
- Memory files persist across context clearing
- Notebook runs without errors

## Notes

- Users will need SDK 0.69.0 (released 2025-09-29) to use `context_management` as a direct parameter
- The demo uses low thresholds suitable for memory operations; production apps with larger tool results should use higher values (3000-5000 tokens)

title: feat(agent-sdk): add session browser cookbook
body:
## What

Adds **notebook 05** to the Agent SDK cookbook series, covering the session management primitives that shipped in `claude-agent-sdk` v0.1.51: `list_sessions`, `get_session_info`, `get_session_messages`, `rename_session`, `tag_session`, `fork_session`, `delete_session`.

The cookbook walks through building a session browser — the sidebar pattern from Claude Code Desktop and the VS Code extension:

1. **Generate demo sessions** — three short `query()` calls with `cwd` scoped to an isolated demo dir (Haiku, single-turn, no tools, to keep token spend minimal)
2. **List and inspect** — `list_sessions()` rendered as a pandas table, `get_session_info()` for single lookup, `get_session_messages()` with content-block extraction
3. **Organize** — `rename_session()`, `tag_session()`, soft-delete via `__hidden` tag + a filter helper
4. **Fork and resume** — `fork_session()` then continue the fork with `query(options=ClaudeAgentOptions(resume=fork.session_id))`
5. **Cleanup** — `delete_session()` over everything the demo created

## Why

Companion cookbook to [session management docs page](https://docs.claude.com/en/agent-sdk/local-session-management). The docs page is reference material; this is the hands-on recipe.

## Also in this PR

- `registry.yaml` — cookbook entry (category: Claude Agent SDK)
- `authors.yaml` — added `qing-ant`
- `claude_agent_sdk/pyproject.toml` — bumped SDK pin from `>=0.0.20` to `>=0.1.51` so the session functions are available in the tutorial kernel
- `claude_agent_sdk/session_browser_demo/.gitkeep` — demo workspace dir for `cwd=`

## Testing

- [x] `ruff check` / `ruff format` pass
- [x] `verify_registry.py` passes (schema + GitHub handle validation)
- [x] pre-commit hooks pass
- [x] Notebook executed end-to-end and outputs committed

The content and explanations are excellent, but the notebook was not run cleanly top-to-bottom before submission, and there's a minor security pattern issue in the memory handler.

<details>
<summary>Actionable Feedback (4 items)</summary>

- [ ] `context_engineering_tools.ipynb` — **Execution counts are not sequential**: counts jump from 11→13 (skipping 12) and then from 15→28 (skipping 16–27). Per cookbook conventions, notebooks must run top-to-bottom without errors with sequential execution counts. Please restart the kernel and run all cells with `Kernel > Restart & Run All`, then re-save.

- [ ] `context_engineering_tools.ipynb` (in the `MemoryToolHandler._resolve` method) — **Path traversal guard uses `startswith` instead of `is_relative_to`**: The check `str(resolved).startswith(str(self.base.resolve()))` has a classic substring false-positive (e.g., `/tmp/memory_abc_sibling/file` would pass if base is `/tmp/memory_abc`). Since the project requires Python 3.11+, use `resolved.is_relative_to(self.base.resolve())` instead. Users copy cookbook patterns — the safe idiom should be shown.

- [ ] `context_engineering_tools.ipynb` (in `MemoryToolHandler._delete`) — `import shutil` should move to the top of the imports cell alongside the other stdlib imports (`json`, `os`, `tempfile`, etc.), not inside the function body.

- [ ] `context_engineering_tools.ipynb` (in `MemoryToolHandler.handle`) — The bare `except Exception as e` catch-all silently converts programming errors (e.g., `KeyError` from a missing `path` key) into ambiguous error strings. Consider catching only `(OSError, ValueError)` and letting unexpected exceptions propagate so they surface clearly during development.

</details>

# Claude Cookbooks

A collection of Jupyter notebooks and Python examples for building with the Claude API.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Install dependencies
uv sync --all-extras

# Install pre-commit hooks
uv run pre-commit install

# Set up API key
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env and add your ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
```

## Development Commands

```bash
make format        # Format code with ruff
make lint          # Run linting
make check         # Run format-check + lint
make fix           # Auto-fix issues + format
make test          # Run pytest
```

Or directly with uv:

```bash
uv run ruff format .           # Format
uv run ruff check .            # Lint
uv run ruff check --fix .      # Auto-fix
uv run pre-commit run --all-files
```

## Code Style

- **Line length:** 100 characters
- **Quotes:** Double quotes
- **Formatter:** Ruff

Notebooks have relaxed rules for mid-file imports (E402), redefinitions (F811), and variable naming (N803, N806).

## Git Workflow

**Branch naming:** `<username>/<feature-description>`

**Commit format (conventional commits):**
```
feat(scope): add new feature
fix(scope): fix bug
docs(scope): update documentation
style: lint/format
```

## Key Rules

1. **API Keys:** Never commit `.env` files. Use `dotenv.load_dotenv()` then access keys via `os.environ` or `os.getenv()`

2. **Dependencies:** Use `uv add <package>` or `uv add --dev <package>`. Never edit pyproject.toml directly.

3. **Models:** Use current Claude models. Check docs.anthropic.com for latest versions.
   - Sonnet: `claude-sonnet-4-6`
   - Haiku: `claude-haiku-4-5`
   - Opus: `claude-opus-4-6`
   - **Never use dated model IDs** (e.g., `claude-sonnet-4-6-20250514`). Always use the non-dated alias.

     - Sonnet 4.5: `anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929-v1:0`
     - Haiku 4.5: `anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0`
     - Prepe

# Issue & Contribution Guidelines

Thank you for your interest in contributing to Claude Cookbooks! This guide helps you understand what types of contributions we welcome and how to make them effectively.

## Before You Open an Issue

1. **Search existing issues** - Your question or bug may already be reported
2. **Check the docs** - Many questions are answered in [Claude Documentation](https://docs.claude.com)
3. **Review CONTRIBUTING.md** - For development setup and contribution guidelines

## Issue Types

### Bug Reports

Use bug reports for:
- Broken links or missing files
- Code that doesn't execute properly
- Incorrect or outdated information
- Missing dependencies or setup issues

**Good bug reports include:**
- Specific file/notebook affected
- Steps to reproduce the issue
- Expected vs actual behavior
- Your environment (Python version, OS)

### Cookbook Proposals

We welcome proposals for new cookbooks that demonstrate Claude's capabilities. Before proposing:

**We're looking for:**
- Notebooks that showcase Claude API/SDK capabilities directly
- Practical, real-world use cases
- Clear educational value with problem-focused learning
- Self-contained examples that users can run immediately

**We generally don't accept:**
- Translations of external frameworks or projects into the SDK
- Notebooks that primarily promote external tools or services
- Content that duplicates existing official documentation
- Proposals without concrete implementation plans

**Strong proposals include:**
1. **Problem statement** - What problem does this solve for users?
2. **Claude capabilities** - Which API/SDK features does this demonstrate?
3. **Learning objectives** - What will users learn?
4. **Outline** - High-level structure of the proposed notebook
5. **Differentiation** - How is this different from existing cookbooks?

- **API/SDK questions**: Check [Claude Documentation](https://docs.claude.com) first
- **General help**: Join our

title: feat(capabilities): add knowledge graph construction guide
body:
Adds `capabilities/knowledge_graph/` — a full guide to building knowledge graphs from unstructured text with Claude.

## What's covered

- **NER + relation extraction** via structured outputs (`client.messages.parse()` with Pydantic models — schema-validated, typed, no regex parsing)
- **Entity resolution** using Claude to cluster surface forms, replacing string-similarity heuristics
- **Graph assembly** in NetworkX with typed nodes and visualization
- **Entity summarization** pooling mentions across documents into structured profiles
- **Multi-hop querying** over serialized subgraphs, with grounded vs ungrounded comparison
- **Evaluation** against a hand-labeled gold set (P/R/F1 for entities and relations)

## Corpus

Six Apollo-program Wikipedia summaries fetched via the Wikipedia REST API — densely interlinked, good for demonstrating dedup.

## Files

- `guide.ipynb` — main tutorial, outputs populated
- `data/sample_triples.json` — gold triples for Apollo 11 and Neil Armstrong articles
- `data/alias_map.json` — shared surface-form canonicalization map
- `evaluation/eval_extraction.py` — standalone P/R/F1 scorer
- `evaluation/README.md` — expected baseline numbers

## Dependencies added

`networkx`, `matplotlib`, `requests` via `uv add`. Bumped `anthropic>=0.77.0` for structured outputs support.

This is a genuinely strong addition — the pedagogy is clear, the dual-model pattern is well-motivated, and the forced tool-use extraction approach is the right technique. However there are several issues to fix before merge.

<details>
<summary>Actionable Feedback (9 items)</summary>

Clear this cell's output. This also signals the notebook was not successfully run end-to-end in a clean environment before commit.

- [ ] `evaluation/eval_extraction.py` (in `main()`, the `wikipedia.summary()` call) — No exception handling around `wikipedia.summary()`. The notebook's fetch loop wraps this in `except wikipedia.exceptions.WikipediaException`, but the eval script does not, so a disambiguation or network error aborts the whole run with a confusing traceback.

- [ ] `evaluation/eval_extraction.py:220-223` — `alias_map` is an identity mapping (`name.lower() → name.lower()`), so `canonicalize()` is a no-op — it just lowercases. The comment says it maps gold names for relation endpoint matching, but it won't help "National Aeronautics and Space Administration" match gold "NASA". Either populate the map with real abbreviation→canonical entries, or remove it and use `.lower()` directly, and update the evaluation README which says "after canonicalization via the gold alias map".

- [ ] `guide.ipynb` and `evaluation/eval_extraction.py` — Neither file calls `load_dotenv()`. Project standard (CLAUDE.md) requires `from dotenv import load_dotenv; load_dotenv()` before accessing `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. The eval README also instructs `export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-...` instead of using `.env`.

- [ ] `guide.ipynb` (introduction cell) — The intro lists implementation steps (table of contents), not learning objectives.

### Summary
This PR adds a well-structured, educational knowledge graph construction guide using Claude for NER, relation extraction, entity resolution, and multi-hop querying. The pedagogical quality and API usage patterns are excellent, but two issues need to be fixed before merge.

<details>
<summary>Actionable Feedback (4 items)</summary>

The standalone `eval_extraction.py` handles this correctly with `Path(__file__).parent`; the notebook should add a note or use a more robust path approach (e.g., `Path().resolve() / "data" / "sample_triples.json"` or a `try/except` with a clear error message).

- [ ] `guide.ipynb` (in cell with `def tool_output(response, name: str) -> dict:`) — The `response` parameter has no type annotation. Add `response: anthropic.types.Message` to make the signature self-documenting.

<summary>Detailed Review</summary>

# Claude Cookbooks

A collection of Jupyter notebooks and Python examples for building with the Claude API.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Install dependencies
uv sync --all-extras

# Install pre-commit hooks
uv run pre-commit install

# Set up API key
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env and add your ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
```

## Development Commands

```bash
make format        # Format code with ruff
make lint          # Run linting
make check         # Run format-check + lint
make fix           # Auto-fix issues + format
make test          # Run pytest
```

Or directly with uv:

```bash
uv run ruff format .           # Format
uv run ruff check .            # Lint
uv run ruff check --fix .      # Auto-fix
uv run pre-commit run --all-files
```

## Code Style

- **Line length:** 100 characters
- **Quotes:** Double quotes
- **Formatter:** Ruff

Notebooks have relaxed rules for mid-file imports (E402), redefinitions (F811), and variable naming (N803, N806).

## Git Workflow

**Branch naming:** `<username>/<feature-description>`

**Commit format (conventional commits):**
```
feat(scope): add new feature
fix(scope): fix bug
docs(scope): update documentation
style: lint/format
```

## Key Rules

1. **API Keys:** Never commit `.env` files. Use `dotenv.load_dotenv()` then access keys via `os.environ` or `os.getenv()`

2. **Dependencies:** Use `uv add <package>` or `uv add --dev <package>`. Never edit pyproject.toml directly.

3. **Models:** Use current Claude models. Check docs.anthropic.com for latest versions.
   - Sonnet: `claude-sonnet-4-6`
   - Haiku: `claude-haiku-4-5`
   - Opus: `claude-opus-4-6`
   - **Never use dated model IDs** (e.g., `claude-sonnet-4-6-20250514`). Always use the non-dated alias.
   - **Bedrock model IDs** follow a different format. Use the base Bedrock model ID from the docs:
     - Opus 4.6: `anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1`
     - Sonnet 4.5: `anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929-v1:0`
     - Haiku 4.5: `anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0`
     - Prepe

title: Remove duplicate model usage check from notebook-review
body:
The `model-check` command already handles model validation for all notebooks and Python files - `notebook-review` doesn't need to do a separate check on models.

model-check triggers on:
```
paths:
  - '**/*.ipynb'
  - '**.py'
  - '**.md'
```

notebook-review triggers on:
```
paths:
  - '**/*.ipynb'
  - 'pyproject.toml'
  - 'uv.lock'
  - 'scripts/**/*.py'
```

So anything model-related that triggers `notebook-review` will also trigger `model-check`
author: StephenGrider

title: ci: add notebook CI/CD with validation and testing
body:
# Add CI/CD Infrastructure for Notebook Quality ## Summary Establishes CI/CD infrastructure for the Anthropic Cookbook with Claude-powered validation and a comprehensive local validation tool. ## What This PR Does - Adds Claude AI workflows for intelligent code review and model validation - Creates `scripts/validate_all_notebooks.py` - interactive validation tool with dashboard and auto-fix - Sets up non-blocking CI to allow incremental fixes - Configures workflows to fetch latest models from [docs.anthropic.com](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models/overview.md) ## Next Steps ```mermaid flowchart TD Start([PR Merged]) --> Validate[Run Full Validation
python scripts/validate_all_notebooks.py] Validate --> AutoFix[Auto-fix Safe Issues
--auto-fix flag] AutoFix --> FixedModels[Deprecated Models Fixed] Validate --> Export[Export Report
--export flag] Export --> CreateIssue[Create Tracking Issue
with categorized problems] CreateIssue --> Prioritize{Prioritize Fixes} Prioritize --> Critical[Critical: Security Issues] Prioritize --> Errors[Execution Errors] Prioritize --> Expand[Expand Coverage
Currently only skills dir] Critical --> PR1[PR: Fix Security Issues
Use os.environ] Errors --> PR2[PR: Fix Execution
Update imports and deps] Expand --> PR3[PR: Add all directories
to CI validation] PR1 --> Strict{All Fixed?} PR2 --> Strict PR3 --> Strict Strict -->|Yes| MakeStrict[Make CI Strict
Remove non-blocking flags] Strict -->|No| MorePRs[Create More PRs] MakeStrict --> Done([CI Fully Enforced]) MorePRs --> Strict style Start fill:#e1f5e1 style Done fill:#e1f5e1 style Critical fill:#ffe1e1 style AutoFix fill:#fff4e1 style MakeStrict fill:#e1e8ff ``` ## Quick Start ```bash # Run interactive validation python scripts/validate_all_notebooks.py # Auto-fix deprecated models python scripts/validate_all_notebooks.py --auto-fix # Generate validation report (keep internal!) python scripts/validate_all_notebooks.py --export > validation_report.md ``` ## Creating Tracking Issues After merge, generate a validation report and create tracking issues: ```bash # Generate report (review before posting publicly!) python scripts/validate_all_notebooks.py --export > report.md # Create internal tracking issue or sanitized public version gh issue create --title "Notebook Improvements Tracking" --body-file report.md ``` ⚠️ **Note**: The validation report may contain sensitive information. Review and sanitize before posting publicly. ## Notes - CI is intentionally non-blocking to allow this infrastructure PR to merge - `validate_notebooks.py` only checks structure (empty cells, error outputs) - `validate_all_notebooks.py` is the comprehensive validation tool with model and security checks - The validation tool identifies opportunities for improvement across the cookbook
